title: Acaulimalva acaulis
intro: Acaulimalva acaulis, also known as Malva acaulis, is a plant species of the Malvaceae family, characterized by its unique, stemless habit and showy, pink or white flowers. Traditionally, Acaulimalva acaulis has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, as well as phenolic acids and triterpenoid saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Acaulimalva acaulis is often used in herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and salves, to take advantage of its therapeutic benefits, although further research is necessary to confirm its efficacy and safety.
